content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance (916)445-8408          Executive Legal          Enforcement
(916) 322 5660                  322-5901         322-6441         322-6448

June 22, 1978

Doctor Howard Holderness
Tahoe Forest Hospital District
Post Office Box 759
Truckee, California 95734

Dear Dr. Holderness:

     Thank you for your letter of December 20, 1977, regarding
the disqualification provisions of the Political Reform Act.
We apologize for the lateness of our response, and hope that
the following advice may be helpful.

     The facts as we understand them are as follows. The
Board of Directors of the Tahoe Forest Hospital District, of
which you are a member, is negotiating a contract to obtain
emergency room physician services from an independent contrac-
tor. One of the issues under consideration in these negotia-
tions is the procedure by which the emergency room physician
may refer patients to other doctors on the hospital staff.
Under the proposed terms of the contract, any member of the
medical staff of the hospital may receive patient referrals
from the emergency room physician. It is our understanding
that you are a member of the hospital's medical staff specia-
lizing in plastic surgery.

     We have been informed that the emergency room physician
has almost total discretion in the exercise of his medical
judgment to determine the physician to whom patients should
be referred. Depending on the type of treatment required, it
is quite possible that a patient could be referred to facili-
ties outside the hospital.

     The question we have been asked to consider is whether
you must disqualify yourself from participating in the
negotiations on the hospital District's contract for emergency
room physician because of your position as a member of the
hospital's medical staff, and the possibility that you will
receive referrals from emergency room physicians.

Dr. Howard Holdemess
June 22, 1978
Page Two

Government Code Section 87100 provides that:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or
in any way attempt to use his official position,
to influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

[emphasis added]

Section 87103(c) states that:

An official has a financial interest in a deci-
sion within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on:

a commercial lending institution in the regular
course of business, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value received by or
promised to the public official within twelve
months prior to the time when the decision is
made...

[emphasis added]

Based on the information set forth in your letter and
subsequent telephone conversations, we conclude that you need
not disqualify yourself from participating in the contract
negotiations in question.

As stated previously, it is our understanding that the
emergency room physician has total discretion with respect to
the number of patients your office would receive and the volume
of business that may be generated by this referral service.
We are led to this conclusion for two reasons. First, the
number of patients that could be referred to your office is
limited by the fact that you practice a specialized form of
medicine - plastic surgery. Second, there are approximately
ten physicians specializing in plastic surgery in the general
area, including hand, breasts and plastic patients requiring plastic surgery could be referred to other
physicians.

Dr. Howard Holderness
June 22, 1978
Page Three


Given these considerations, we conclude that it is not
foreseeable within the meaning of the Political Reform Act
that your financial interest would be materially affected by
participating in the hospital negotiations for emergency room
physicians. I hope that this advice has been helpful. If you
have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Dwight Dickerson
Legal Counsel
Conflicts of Interest Division

DED:TP:mfa

cc: Gordon Simonds

Joel Grey
Tahoe Forest Hospital District

TAHOE
FOREST
HOSPITAL

Tahoe Forest Hospital District
P.O. Box 759        Truckee, California 95734
Telephone · 916·587·3541

December 20, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street, 2nd Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen,

     I am an elected member of the Board of Directors of Tahoe
Forest Hospital District which is organized, incorporated and
managed pursuant to "The Local Hospital District Law" as contained
in Health and Safety Code §32000 et seq.  The District owns and
operates a general acute care hospital located at Truckee, California
which is licensed by the State Department of Health.  I am a member
of the medical staff of that hospital--i.e., Tahoe Forest Hospital.

     As provided for in Government Code § 83114(b), I want to take
this opportunity to request the written advice of the Commission
regarding the following question:

     Does an elected member of the board of directors of a state
agency, as that term is defined in Government Code § 82049,
have a conflict of interest as defined in Government Code
§§ 87100 and 87103 if, as a duly appointed member of a sub-
committee of such board, he participates in the negotiation,
recommendation, and approval of a contract with an independent
contractor who, by the terms of said contract, is to provide
emergency room physician services to the state agency, if such
board member, in his independent status as a member of the
medical staff of a hospital owned and operated by the state
agency, may receive--as may all members of the medical staff--
patient referrals from the emergency room physicians during
the course of the contract term inasmuch as one of the issues
in the contract negotiations is the procedure for patient
referrals of persons treated by the emergency room physicians
and under the proposed contract terms any medical staff member
of the hospital may receive patient referrals from the emergency
room physicians if the medical staff member is the personal
physician of a specific patient or if he places his name on the
emergency call list maintained by the hospital?

Page 2

     By way of elaboration, I should add that if such contract is
adopted by the parties, it is my intention to place my name on the
hospital's emergency call list and accept referrals by being on
such list, and to accept referrals of private patients of mine who
have been treated by an emergency room physician in the hospital's
emergency room.

     In addition, I should note that I have been advised by Legal
Counsel to the District that the State Department of Health regulations
governing licensure of general acute care hospitals provide in perti-
nent part in Title 22, California Administrative Code § 70717(c):

     (c)  Patients shall be admitted only upon referral and under the
care of a physician or dentist who shall be a member of the
medical staff of the hospital.  The patient's condition and pro-
visional diagnosis shall be established on admission by the
patient's physician or by the physician in residence who admits
the patient. (Emphasis added.)

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Howard Holderness, Jr., M.D.
                                        Member, Board of Directors
                                        Tahoe Forest Hospital District

cc:  Board of Directors
     Tahoe Forest Hospital District
